reportingProfile.description: Reporting profile for resource-variation comparison across provider-backed K3s clusters.

baseline.purpose: Provider-backed baseline for LocalAI worker-mode characterization on a proxmox-k3s-generated K3s cluster.
selectionCriteria: Keep the provider-backed baseline intentionally close to the historical application-level baseline so that infrastructure effects are not mixed with model, workload, or placement changes.
Use two RPC workers to preserve the validated LocalAI worker-mode topology before introducing resource, node-count, or placement variation cycles.
Use the smallest validated model family member to minimize footprint during the first provider-backed end-to-end cycle.
Use the controlled low-load workload to validate the full provider-backed pipeline before executing broader sweeps.
Use the explicit PL_COLOCATED placement profile for the first provider-backed baseline; later cycles can switch placement profiles while keeping model, workload, and infrastructure fixed.
lockRules: Do not mutate B1 after it has been used for a frozen provider-backed cycle.
Do not reuse B0 artifacts as evidence for B1.
Do not interpret B1 and B0 results as directly interchangeable without accounting for their different infrastructure profiles.
Use B1 as the provider-backed reference only after the declared proxmox-k3s cluster has passed validation.
Collect minimal observability evidence for B1 using metrics-server, kubectl snapshots and Locust artifacts before diagnosis and reporting.
baseline.notes: B1 intentionally inherits the application-level shape of B0 while switching to the INFRA_C1_1CP_2W_8C16G provider-backed infrastructure profile. This keeps the first provider-backed cycle focused on validating the provisioning-to-benchmark path rather than changing multiple dimensions at once.
baseline.scenarioProfileName: Provider-backed baseline for LocalAI worker-mode characterization on a proxmox-k3s-generated K3s cluster.

payload.description: Comparative provider-backed campaign that varies worker CPU and memory capacity while preserving the same LocalAI workload, model, worker count and placement policy.

scope.primaryQuestion: How do worker CPU and memory capacity affect LocalAI worker-mode latency, throughput and resource pressure when model, workload, worker count and placement remain fixed?

stateModel.profileStatus: Configuration-profile lifecycle state. This is the canonical meaning of the backward-compatible status field.
stateModel.configurationState: Whether the cycle configuration is active, locked, superseded, or deprecated.
stateModel.executionState: Latest known execution outcome for the cycle or campaign.
stateModel.status: Backward-compatible alias of profileStatus; execution outcomes must be read from executionState.

infrastructureSummarySemantics.description: Each campaign variant resolves its own infrastructure profile and provider binding; cycle-level reporting must aggregate variant infrastructure metadata.

payload.placementIntent: Place the LocalAI server and active RPC workers on the same worker node to minimize intra-application communication distance.
payload.researchQuestion: How does a compact LocalAI topology behave when communication distance is minimized but CPU and memory contention may increase?

selectorPolicy.rationale: Stable node names are retained for compatibility with the current Kustomize overlays; label-based node-pool selection is the preferred direction for future generated clusters.

serverPlacement.placementNotes: The current server manifest is still node-name compatible; later topology profiles can replace this with a label-based selector.

mappingSemantics.activeWorkerNodeMapByWorkerCount: Application-level worker-count mapping currently defined for W1, W2, W3 and W4, with all active RPC workers co-located on genai-pb-worker-02.

compositionSelectionPolicy.selectionRationale: This placement profile varies the LocalAI application topology by active RPC-worker count. Infrastructure worker-node count is not the primary selector for this profile.

expectedBenefits: lower communication distance between server and RPC workers
expectedRisks: higher CPU and memory contention on the selected node

governance.comparisonPolicy: Placement comparisons must keep infrastructure, model, workload, worker count, protocol, and metric collection fixed unless a cycle profile explicitly declares otherwise.

payload.description: Runtime-generated provider-backed variant cycle for RV_4C16G within the C2 campaign.

scenario.purpose: Resource-variation benchmark variant using 4 vCPU / 16 GiB per worker while keeping model, workload, LocalAI worker count and placement unchanged.
selectionCriteria: Vary only the worker-node CPU and memory shape so the resulting measurements remain attributable to infrastructure capacity changes.
Keep the LocalAI model fixed to llama-3.2-1b-instruct:q4_k_m to avoid mixing model-size effects with infrastructure effects.
Keep the workload fixed to the controlled low-load profile to preserve comparability across resource shapes.
Keep two LocalAI RPC workers and the co-located placement policy fixed so the campaign evaluates resource capacity rather than topology or worker-count changes.
Execute each variant through the same provider-backed lifecycle: provisioning, validation, deployment, benchmark, diagnosis, reporting, completion gate and freeze.
lockRules: Do not change model, workload, LocalAI worker count or placement inside this resource-variation campaign.
Do not compare this scenario with another scenario unless the infrastructure profile and provider binding are explicitly reported.
Treat unsupported or non-schedulable variants as methodological evidence rather than discarding them silently.
Keep provider-generated kubeconfig files and local provider configurations out of version control.
Run campaign-level diagnosis, reporting, completion-gate evaluation and freeze only after all declared variants have been attempted or explicitly marked unsupported.
scenario.notes: This scenario belongs to the C2 resource-variation campaign and changes only the declared worker-node resource shape: 4 vCPU / 16 GiB per worker.
scenario.comparisonPolicy: Only CPU and memory capacity are varied in this scenario. Model, workload, LocalAI worker count and placement remain fixed.

templateMetadata.generationPolicy: Only explicit placeholders delimited by double underscores may be substituted. Runtime generation must not perform global literal replacements such as C1 or B1.
